message:
fix(net): websocket close handshake, fallible mask generation, IPv6 handling, and accept storm backoff

WebSocket close handshake (src/net/websocket/close.rs):
- Add mark_response_sent() method that transitions CloseReceived →
  Closed. Previously there was no way to signal that the response close
  frame was actually written to the wire, leaving the handshake stuck in
  CloseReceived. The caller can now mark completion after the frame
  codec confirms the write.
- Add test verifying the full receive-close → mark-response-sent → closed
  transition.
- Remove spurious blank lines.

WebSocket mask key generation (src/net/websocket/frame.rs):
- Change generate_mask_key() from returning [u8; 4] (panicking on RNG
  failure) to returning io::Result<[u8; 4]>. The previous code used
  .expect("OS RNG unavailable") which would panic the entire runtime if
  getrandom failed (e.g., early boot on Linux before /dev/urandom is
  seeded). Now the error propagates as a normal IO error through the
  codec's encode path.

WebSocket handshake IPv6 (src/net/websocket/handshake.rs):
- Handle unbracketed IPv6 addresses (containing multiple colons) by
  treating the entire host_port as the host with the default port,
  rather than misinterpreting colons as host:port separators. Bracketed
  IPv6 [::1]:8080 was already handled correctly.

TCP listener accept storm backoff (src/net/tcp/listener.rs):
- Unify the backoff delay logic: when note_accept_would_block() returns
  a storm backoff duration, use it even when the reactor is armed (not
  just in fallback poll mode). Previously, reactor-armed mode always
  returned Pending with zero delay, but accept storms can cause rapid
  spurious wakeups even with reactor notification. Now the delay is
  max(FALLBACK_ACCEPT_BACKOFF, storm_backoff) for fallback mode, and
  storm_backoff for reactor mode when it exceeds the base threshold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
